text: A CDN is a geographically distributed network of edge servers that cache and serve content from locations closest to users, reducing latency and origin server load

A CDN is a geographically distributed network of edge servers that cache and serve content from locations closest to users, reducing latency and origin server load. Major CDNs: Cloudflare, Akamai, Fastly, Amazon CloudFront. CDNs handle ~75% of all web traffic.
CDN features: DDoS protection, SSL/TLS termination, WAF, image optimization, edge computing (Cloudflare Workers, AWS Lambda@Edge).
CDN edge locations: Cloudflare 330+ cities, CloudFront 600+ PoPs.
